item.title: Education Makes All The Difference
item.description:
Penetration not required for anal; some people just like the external stimulation. Also, a good diagram at the bottom, here. Feel free to zoom in.
Orgasms are great, but there's lots of other great things to enjoy, too. Don't get in your head about having to orgasm every time. And as always, please feel free to share your takeaways, anecdotes and preferences. Conversation is important.
item.dc:creator: AdamTheHedonist

1
2

5

8

20
21
22
23
24
25
26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: This is by @Hybx This one is from last year. They have a lot of newer ones in their posts but I liked this one most. Go have a look
27 Man Tax: Matthew Lewis
“You may say I'm a dreamer, but I'm not the only one; I hope some day you'll join us, and the world will live as one.” - John Lennon I try to keep my posts "good vibe zones". So, instead of finding the one thing in here you don't like and commenting on it, maybe let it go, don't comment on it and find something that you do like. Seriously y'all. Coming to only comment "omg that's AI" bums me out. Love you long time. So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: COVID Vaccine Access Now. Here's Who Can Get A Shot This Fall—And Where You Can Get It
item.description: The FDA updated its guidance. The agency approved updated COVID shots for this season, but limited who can receive them. The FDA has updated its latest COVID vaccine guidance to say that only people 65 and older or those at high risk (of any age) should get the vaccine or boosters, but the CDC still needs to weigh in here. The FDA has authorized COVID vaccines for use in these particular age groups: Moderna: High-risk people aged 6 months and up Pfizer: High-risk people aged 5 years and up Novavax: High-risk people aged 12 years and up People in the "high risk" group includes those 65 and above, and anyone with an underlying health condition. https://www.cdc.gov/covid/hcp/clinical-care/underlying-conditions.html Kennedy revoked the guidance recommending pregnant people get boosters. However, Kennedy did say in a note on X that “these vaccines are available for all patients who choose them after consulting with their doctors.” Overall, Dr. Adalja says that “any person with a high risk condition is ‘eligible’ for a COVID vaccination.” As for everyone else, it’s not really clear at this moment. Where can I get a COVID booster or vaccine? Here’s where things get complicated. For starters, 16 states require CDC recommendations in order for pharmacists to administer the COVID vaccines. (The other 34 states abide by whatever the FDA authorizes.) "That’s a problem,” Dr. Russo says. “For those 16 states, 13 of them will administer the vaccine with a physician’s prescription." Given the fact that most people get their COVID vaccines at pharmacies, this is an important sticking point and potential barrier to getting the vaccine, he adds. However, you should be able to eventually get the vaccine at your local pharmacy if you qualify—but you may need a prescription. Is the vaccine still free? Remember, the vaccine and boosters have historically been free since they started being administered in 2021. So whether people will have to pay out of pocket is also not totally clear right now. “Health insurance coverage for those in the eligible group should be intact, but for others it may vary depending on the insurance company,” Dr. Adalja says. People who don’t have health insurance coverage will have to pay between $140 and $160 per vaccine, according to Dr. Russo. “That’s a lot of money for most people,” he says. “Commercial insurances will cover whatever the CDC recommends,” Dr. Russo adds. “But there’s a lot of uncertainty.” https://www.womenshealthmag.com/health/a65969383/covid-vaccine-access-eligibility-new-variant https://www.axios.com/2025/08/29/cvs-covid-vaccines-kennedy-rfk Oregon, Washington, California form health care alliance to protect vaccine access https://www.opb.org/article/2025/09/03/vaccines-oregon-washington-california-cdc/ Massachusetts becomes first state to impose its own vaccine coverage rules https://www.axios.com/2025/09/04/massachusetts-vaccine-coverage-rules
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: GovBrief #217
item.description: Two Cabinet secretaries, Marco Rubio (State) and Pete Hegseth (Defense), told the world today that America can summarily execute people on drug suspicions in international waters. And that still was not the day’s most shocking news. The pot is at a rolling boil. Authoritarian moves are spilling over every edge and scalding public life. The only way to cool it down is collective resistance. Don’t leave truth to the algorithm. Our free and private nightly email arms you with images, data, and quotes so you can see it all for yourself. Add your email at govbrief dot today or on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com [1] https://go.govbrief.today/la-guard-order-paused [2] https://go.govbrief.today/dc-files-suit-feds [3] https://go.govbrief.today/rubio-kill-drug-smugglers [4] https://go.govbrief.today/chicago-area-base-approved [5] https://go.govbrief.today/european-security-funds-cut [6] https://go.govbrief.today/rfk-senate-health-hearing [7] https://go.govbrief.today/ma-vaccine-insurer-requirement [8] https://go.govbrief.today/un-iran-costco-restriction [9] https://go.govbrief.today/transgender-mental-health-guns [10] https://go.govbrief.today/palestinian-ngo-icc-sanctions [11] https://go.govbrief.today/judge-rules-recission-illegal [12] https://go.govbrief.today/syracuse-plant-immigration-raid [13] https://go.govbrief.today/doj-cook-investigation-subpoena [14] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-tariffs-appeal-scotus [15] https://go.govbrief.today/fed-nominee-keep-job [16] https://go.govbrief.today/venezuela-buzzes-navy-ships [17] https://go.govbrief.today/defendant-deported-dugan-case [18] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-ftc-appeal-scotus [19] https://go.govbrief.today/everglades-center-remains-open [20] https://go.govbrief.today/hundreds-arrested-immigration-raid [21] https://go.govbrief.today/doj-boston-sanctuary-law [22] https://go.govbrief.today/33k-other-employees-ice [23] https://go.govbrief.today/schill-northwestern-president-resigns [24] https://go.govbrief.today/burchett-involved-physical-altercation [25] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-cancel-airline-refund [26] https://go.govbrief.today/nyc-mayor-adams-sliwa [27] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-911-museumleadership [28] https://go.govbrief.today/smithsonian-team-content-trump [29] https://go.govbrief.today/schiff-propublica-cabinet-mortgages [30] https://go.govbrief.today/sniper-no-bid-contract [31] https://go.govbrief.today/james-trump-penalty-appeal [32] https://go.govbrief.today/dc-judge-blasts-prosecutors Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Pace yourselves. This is our second biggest day ever in terms of volume, and we've done 85 stories in 3 days. Today will be more of the same. The jobs numbers look horrible, Trump's renaming Defense to War today, and the world likely has something to say about us declaring we're allowed to summarily execute people in international waters.
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos
